# IC Eight

> IC Eight helps SaaS teams design Japanese product UX and AI-native localization workflows from product intent, not sentence-by-sentence translation.

IC Eight works with global SaaS teams entering or improving their presence in the Japanese market. The work focuses on Japanese UX language, onboarding, support communication, lifecycle content, localization strategy, and AI-native workflows for generating and evaluating Japanese product content.

IC Eight does not treat Japanese localization as a file-delivery task. It treats the Japanese layer as part of the product experience.

## Core approach

IC Eight uses the AI-Native Localization Framework: a workflow for generating Japanese UX and content from intent, product context, constraints, validation criteria, and human judgment.

Intent-Based Generation starts from the content's intent — its purpose or goal — not from translating the English sentence line by line.

The goal is not natural-sounding Japanese in isolation. The goal is Japanese product communication that works in context.

This is different from "AI localization" in the common sense (using AI to automate and speed up translation at scale). AI-Native Localization Framework does not automate translation — it replaces the translation step itself, generating the target language from intent rather than translating source text.

## Key concepts

- AI-Native Localization Framework
- Intent-Based Generation
- Japanese UX localization
- Product-facing Japanese
- Localization as workflow design
- Japanese onboarding and support communication
- AI-generated Japanese content evaluation
- Japan market entry for SaaS
